
The basic idea is that
1. We'd like to have fidelity at the vuln severity level.
   A critical vuln should definitely be non-compliant immediately
   (in a live scan) and prevent a deployment (in a gate).
   But it seems reasonable that a low one should be handled differently.
2. We would like the resolution of low level vulns and regular
   feature development to be orthogonal in terms of branches/pr/etc.

o) If the cat is in the Env-policy
   then you have to do cat when there are NO vulns.

o) If the cat is in the Env-policy
   then the cat has to ALSO be made in the repos main workflow 
   before the kosli-attest gate for the target environment 

o) To know how long a vuln has not been fixed I am
   using the Trail creation timestamp. 

o) You do the snyk scan WITHOUT using a .snyk policy file 
   so that all vulns are reported.
   A simple python program merges any expiry timestamps 
   for matching vulns into the cat json data.

o) The snyk-container-test returns a non-zero exit code when it finds
   vulnerabilities. This must not cause the step/job to fail, since:
   - we want to do an attestation for each vuln
   - we are using strategy:matrix and failures ripples to other jobs

o) You need separate cats for vulns in beta vs vulns in prod
   if you want the rules to be different. Eg a vuln can remain
   green in beta longer than in prod.
   You also need a way to prevent the cats for beta affecting 
   the compliance in prod and vice-versa.

MISC

o) git-checkout is required for kosli-begin-trail
   Is that intentional?

o) Not obvious what the evaluation of the jq rules is in the attestation.

o) Not obvious, from docs, what the exit-code of kosli-attest-custom is.
   If the attestation is false, it does not exit non-zero 
   (which is correct and useful), but this is not stated explicitly
   in the commands page on https://docs.kosli.com
